title: Master Plan Amendment for Conformance
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 27, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Lauren Knox, Senior Planner
DEPT: Development Services
SUBJECT: Master Plan Amendment for Conformance

Background
A master plan amendment case (LDC26-00043) will be going before the Planning Commission on April 1st, 2026, to address conformance issues with the updated Truckee Meadows Regional Plan, and to address state law changes from the 2025 Legislative Session. Master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 will be processed consistent with how similar master plan amendments were processed in the past, but Lauren Knox, Senior Planner, wanted to ensure the Mayor and City Council was informed of master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 before the Planning Commission hearing. The amendments are intended to be targeted to address the conformance items. Master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 is not intended to be a full-scale update to the ReImagine Reno Master Plan.

Discussion
A brief description of the proposed amendments are as follows:

Regional Plan Conformance
As part of the 2024 Truckee Meadows Regional Plan Update, each jurisdictional master plan must be submitted to the Truckee Meadows Regional Planning Agency (TMRPA) for a conformance review to identify if any changes are needed to the master plan following the update to the Regional Plan per NRS 278.028. TMRPA staff has reviewed the ReImagine Reno Master Plan and found the ReImagine Reno Master Plan in conformance with the Regional Plan with a list of scheduled additions. The proposed amendments to the ReImagine Reno Master Plan address this list as follows:

• Amendment to include recognition of Natural Resource Consideration Areas (NRCA) found in Policy NR4 of the Regional Plan.

• Amendment to reference and utilize the "Integrated Source Water and 319(h) Watershed Protection Plan for Public Water Systems and the Truckee River in the Truckee Meadows" to promote consideration of critical source water protection areas.

• Amendment to include references to the Truckee Meadows Trails Plan as a resource.

• All maps throughout the ReImagine Reno Master Plan depicting the City of Reno's Sphere of Influence (SOI) have been amended to reflect the new SOI.

State Law Conformance
Assembly Bill 540 (AB 540) altered the way that affordable housing is defined in state law by expanding the housing affordability tiers and referring to the new structure as attainable housing. Certain policies and narratives were amended to better incorporate state law changes requiring the ReImagine Reno Master Plan to address the broader concept of "attainable housing," while still recognizing that affordable housing requires added focus due to community needs and the federal and state funding differences and references.

Assembly Bill 96 (AB96) requires heat mitigation to be addressed in the ReImagine Reno Master Plan. The ReImagine Reno Master Plan already includes many policies regarding urban heat, sustainable development, and climate change, which all provide policy direction related to heat mitigation. Certain policies were amended to further enhance heat mitigation needs, and a new "Planning for Heat" callout narrative was included to describe the overarching heat mitigation concepts and provides a guide to key policies that impact each interaction. A new policy addition, Policy 6.2L, and accompanying implementation plan measure is proposed to target the discussion regarding access to cooling spaces and public drinking water, as required by AB 96.

Future Amendments Anticipated
Though master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 is intended to be limited and targeted to key conformance updates that keep the ReImagine Reno Master Plan aligned with the Regional Plan and state law, through the master plan amendment process Lauren Knox, Senior Planner, has recognized that a more full-scale update to the ReImagine Reno Master Plan may be needed. Lauren Knox, Senior Planner, believes a more full-scale update to the ReImagine Reno Master Plan will be more appropriate following adoption of the new Strategic Plan and with additional time. The proposed amendments discussed in this staff report were necessary to be completed promptly due to state law and regional planning-related deadlines.

Next Steps
Once master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 is reviewed by the Planning Commission, master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 will come before the City Council for approval before going to the Truckee Meadows Regional Planning Agency (TMRPA) for a conformance review. Lauren Knox, Senior Planner, anticipates that the City Council will hear master plan amendment case LDC26-00043 on May 6th, 2026. The proposed redline changes and additional information will be included in the materials that will go before the City Council.

fields.tldr: Senior Planner Lauren Knox is informing the Mayor and City Council of a targeted master plan amendment (LDC26-00043) before its April 1, 2026 Planning Commission hearing. The amendments update the ReImagine Reno Master Plan to conform with the 2024 Truckee Meadows Regional Plan Update and 2025 state law changes from AB 540 (attainable housing) and AB 96 (heat mitigation). This is not a full-scale master plan overhaul; a more comprehensive update is anticipated after adoption of the new Strategic Plan. Following Planning Commission review, the item is expected to come before City Council on May 6, 2026, before proceeding to TMRPA for conformance review.
fields.key_stats: 2 state bills addressed: AB 540 (attainable housing definitions) and AB 96 (heat mitigation requirements); 4 regional plan conformance amendments including updated Sphere of Influence maps and new NRCA recognition; 1 new policy (Policy 6.2L) proposed for cooling spaces and public drinking water access.
fields.key_decisions: Council is being informed ahead of the Planning Commission hearing; no Council action is required at this stage. Future Council approval of LDC26-00043 is anticipated on May 6, 2026, after which the amendment proceeds to TMRPA for conformance review.
fields.action_items: 1. Planning Commission to review LDC26-00043 on April 1, 2026. 2. City Council to hear and approve LDC26-00043 on approximately May 6, 2026. 3. Following Council approval, submit LDC26-00043 to TMRPA for conformance review. 4. Lauren Knox to pursue a full-scale ReImagine Reno Master Plan update after adoption of the new Strategic Plan.

projects: Integrated Source Water and 319(h) Watershed Protection Plan for Public Water Systems and the Truckee River in the Truckee Meadows
